plannerRationale.agencyDesign: Four baseline measurement nodes plus Mystery choice plus locked Quest/Boss destinations. Silent letter group receives two measurements: partial_visual_recall Word Radar for scaffolded practice, then audio-prompted Spell Check for independent construction evidence. High-frequency group receives visible_read Word Radar for recognition evidence, then Pronunciation for fluency evidence. Mystery provides preference data and recovery after baseline work. Quest destination is locked and targets silent_letters group for generated transfer after baseline evidence supports it. Boss destination is locked until Quest evidence confirms mastery readiness. This structure respects the two-group source distinction, measures each group by its declared purpose, and creates the evidence spine needed for transfer theory and Quest unlock by session 2.
plannerRationale.evidenceDesign: Reina's homework separates silent letter spelling construction from high-frequency word recognition. The silent letter group requires independent spelling recall, while the high-frequency group requires automatic recognition and fluency. With 2 days until test and no prior evidence, today's session must establish a clean baseline for both groups, identify any weak targets, and prepare a transfer theory for Quest unlock.
plannerRationale.layoutChoice: Sunny materialized the horizontal adventure board from the planner's validated intervention node plan.

shown/chosen/skipped/completed outcome for pronunciation; preference only

evidenceUsed.summary: Benchmark Advance Unit 9 Week 3: two groups with distinct purposes (silent letter spelling vs high-frequency recognition)
Test date 2026-05-29, 2 days remaining, urgent intensity, 3 expected sessions
Reina, grade 2, no recent evidence on file, horizontal adventure spine preference
Board generated from stored source assignment and local OCR/text extraction.

openQuestions: Which silent letter pattern (kn-, -gn, wr-) will require the most support?
Will Reina show spelling confidence or hesitation on 'know' vs 'write'?
Are the high-frequency words already automatized or will they need fluency practice?
Does Reina prefer visible scaffolding or independent recall challenges?

planTheory.hypothesis: Reina's homework separates silent letter spelling construction from high-frequency word recognition. The silent letter group requires independent spelling recall, while the high-frequency group requires automatic recognition and fluency. With 2 days until test and no prior evidence, today's session must establish a clean baseline for both groups, identify any weak targets, and prepare a transfer theory for Quest unlock.
evidenceSummary: Source document shows two teacher-labeled groups with distinct instructional purposes
Silent Letters group (sign, know, write) carries orthographic pattern focus and Benchmark Advance spelling production expectation
High-Frequency Words group (among, building, circle) carries automaticity and recognition focus, not pattern analysis
Mastery context shows 2 days until test, urgent intensity, 3 expected sessions remaining
Child chart shows no recent evidence, so today is first baseline for this homework
Grade 2 learner with horizontal adventure spine preference and companion Matilda
planTheory.intervention: Four baseline measurement nodes plus Mystery choice plus locked Quest/Boss destinations. Silent letter group receives two measurements: partial_visual_recall Word Radar for scaffolded practice, then audio-prompted Spell Check for independent construction evidence. High-frequency group receives visible_read Word Radar for recognition evidence, then Pronunciation for fluency evidence. Mystery provides preference data and recovery after baseline work. Quest destination is locked and targets silent_letters group for generated transfer after baseline evidence supports it. Boss destination is locked until Quest evidence confirms mastery readiness. This structure respects the two-group source distinction, measures each group by its declared purpose, and creates the evidence spine needed for transfer theory and Quest unlock by session 2.
supportCriteria: Silent letter words (sign, know, write) all correct on first try in both Word Radar and Spell Check with low latency and no retries
High-frequency words (among, building, circle) all recognized and pronounced fluently with no hesitation
Reina completes all four baseline nodes with positive affect and engages willingly with Mystery choice
Evidence snapshot shows clean baseline across both groups, allowing Quest unlock for transfer proof next session
reviseCriteria: One or two silent letter words require retry or show hesitation in Word Radar or Spell Check; partial mastery suggests need for targeted pattern support next session
One or two high-frequency words show hesitation or mispronunciation; mixed automaticity suggests need for pronunciation practice or repeated exposure
Reina shows fatigue or avoidance signals during baseline work; dosage or pacing should adjust for session 2
Evidence snapshot identifies exact weak targets; session 2 should give those weak targets strictly more academic support than mastered targets
falsifyCriteria: Two or more silent letter words missed or heavily struggled across both Word Radar and Spell Check; widespread pattern confusion requires explicit silent letter teaching before transfer
Two or more high-frequency words missed, skipped, or heavily hesitated; recognition gap requires phonics support or visible scaffolding before fluency work
Reina avoids baseline work, shows frustration, or abandons session; motivation drop requires smaller dosage, more scaffolding, or different activity sequence
Baseline evidence is too contradictory or incomplete to support a Quest theory; session 2 should probe the contradictory targets before Quest unlock

plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: partial_visual_recall spelling practice with letter-by-letter input
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: All three words completed first-try with low latency and no retries; Reina shows confidence with silent letter patterns
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: One or two words require retry or show hesitation; mixed confidence suggests partial mastery and need for targeted support
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Two or more words missed or skipped; heavy retry usage; frustration signals; need audio-cued or visible scaffolding before independent recall

plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: audio-prompted spelling construction with hidden target
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: All three words spelled correctly from audio prompt without visible support; confirms independent spelling readiness
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: One word requires retry or shows silent letter error; theory should add targeted pattern support for that exact word
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Two or more spelling errors; persistent silent letter confusion; need to drop back to visible pattern teaching or letter-by-letter scaffolding

plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: visible_read recognition and fluency with whole-word voice response
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: All three words read fluently with fast correct responses and no hesitation; confirms automatic recognition
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: One word shows hesitation, mispronunciation, or retry; mixed automaticity suggests need for pronunciation practice
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Two or more words missed, skipped, or heavily hesitated; recognition gap requires phonics support or repeated exposure before fluency work

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: All three words pronounced correctly with natural fluency; no decoding struggle; confirms high-frequency automaticity
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: One word mispronounced or hesitated; pronunciation gap suggests need for supported read-aloud practice
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Two or more pronunciation errors or heavy decoding struggle; need phonics review or chunk-by-chunk teaching before fluency dosage

plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: preference and choice signal after baseline work
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Reina engages willingly; chooses activity and completes it; positive affect and recovery
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Reina skips or replays; neutral engagement; preference signal is weak or unclear
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Reina avoids choice, shows frustration, or abandons Mystery; motivation drop requires smaller baseline dosage or more scaffolding next session

plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: locked destination; will unlock after baseline evidence supports transfer theory
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Baseline shows all silent letter words correct with confidence; Quest unlocks for generated transfer proof
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Baseline shows one or two silent letter misses; Quest should unlock after targeted support next session
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Baseline shows widespread silent letter confusion; Quest remains locked; next session should teach pattern explicitly before transfer

plannedMeasurements.evidenceType: locked destination; will unlock after Quest evidence confirms transfer readiness
plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Quest evidence shows independent transfer success across both groups; Boss unlocks for final mastery gate
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Quest evidence shows partial success; Boss should unlock after targeted support for weak targets
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Quest evidence shows transfer failure; Boss remains locked; next session should reteach failed skill and retry Quest loop
